Off Calendar Jan-23-2019 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-16-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Kithas for David Demars and Chanel Clayton.

Mr. Kithas stated that he has a proposed order, that he is asking the Court to distribute to Vernell McGee as Trustee and that the will distributes everything to Vernell. The Court pointed out that it is not dealing with the Trust. Mr. Kithas stated that nothing was opened for Edmund Dohart (Decedent's Husband), that Mr. Dohart wanted to get the house to Mr. McGee outside of probate, that the husband gets one third, that the Children get two thirds and that all of the Beneficiaries in Ms. Jackson's estate are alright with it going to Mr. McGee. The Court suggests doing an 850 Petition to get it into the Trust. The Court informed Mr. Kithas that the money should not go into the Attorney trust account, that it goes against the local rule and that the Cort frowns upon it. Mr. Kithas stated that he will file an ex parte petition to keep the funds in the Attorney trust account. The Waiver of Account and Report of Administrator, etc. is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-19-2017 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jun-07-2017 Continued To Jul-19-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Kithas and Qian Li Yang for and with Chanel Clayton and for David Demars (Petitioners).
Dan Clayton (Husband of Chanel) also present.

The Court noted that the Fiduciaries have not signed and verified the petition. Mr. Kithas stated that the petition got kicked back, that he signed the declaration because he was the one who had the conversation with Mr. McGee and that there was no indication in the Examiner's notes that the petition needed to be signed. Mr. Kithas also stated that the amended petition went out with notice, but the notice did not say amended, and that he called the three Brothers to advise them that it was the same petition being heard on a different day. The Court informed Counsel that his Clients need to sign all of their own pleadings and stated that it makes sense to file an amended notice. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to July 19, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.
